channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kirinyaga Governor Disburses KSh 129.5 Million in Bursaries and Supports Education Holistically
item.description: Kirinyaga Governor Anne Waiguru has disbursed a total of KSh 129.5 million in bursaries to 48,000 students across the county, aiming to ensure no child stays home due to lack of fees. Since 2017, Waig...

item.title: Centre Issues Guidelines to Empower School Management Committees Across India
item.description: In a significant step to decentralise school governance and align with the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, the Ministry of Education has issued new comprehensive guidelines mandating the formati...

item.title: A Photographer’s Perspective on Growing Up During the War in Donbas
item.description: Alina Panasenko, a young Ukrainian photographer, grew up in Severodonetsk amid the occupation of Donetsk and Luhansk regions. Her teenage years were marked by constant shelling and political instabili...

item.title: India Calls for Global Cooperation on Big Cat Conservation Ahead of First International Summit
item.description: India is set to host the inaugural International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA) Summit in Delhi on June 1-2, 2026, and has called upon all big cat range countries that are not yet members to join this treaty...

item.title: Comelec Approves UBJP to Join BARMM Elections Despite Objections
item.description: The Commission on Elections (Comelec) has officially accredited the United Bangsamoro Justice Party (UBJP) as a regional parliamentary political party, allowing it to participate in the upcoming Septe...

item.title: Kyiv Monuments Brought to Life Through Poetry and Light in Spanish Artist’s Performance
item.description: Three prominent monuments in Kyiv—the statues of Volodymyr the Great in Volodymyrska Hill Park, Bohdan Khmelnytsky on Sophia Square, and Hryhorii Skovoroda at Kontraktova Square—were illuminated and e...

item.title: Quebec Court of Appeal Hears Request for Retrial in Case of Man Convicted of Antisemitism
item.description: A Quebec man, Gabriel Sohier-Chaput, who was convicted in 2023 for promoting hatred against Jewish people, is seeking a retrial at the Quebec Court of Appeal. Sohier-Chaput, represented by lawyer Anto...

item.title: Google Chrome for Android Introduces Option to Share Approximate Location
item.description: Google has updated Chrome on Android to allow users to share their approximate location with websites rather than their exact location. This new feature gives users greater control over their location...

item.title: Income tax cuts and superannuation changes to take effect from July amid upcoming budget reforms
item.description: From 1 July 2026, Australians will see key changes in income tax rates and superannuation rules, as previously legislated measures come into effect. The lowest income tax bracket will drop from 16% to...

item.title: Labour braces for local election results as Starmer stays low-profile
item.description: Ahead of the local elections, Keir Starmer, Prime Minister and leader of the Labour Party, has remained conspicuously low-profile on the campaign trail, with Downing Street advisers warning that his p...

item.title: Health Minister Confirms South African Rats Are Not Carriers of Hantavirus
item.description: Health Minister Dr Aaron Motsoaledi addressed Parliament regarding concerns about hantavirus following international cases. The recent fatalities involved a Dutch woman who died at OR Tambo Airport an...

item.title: CS Barasa Directs NEMA to Enforce Environmental Laws Strictly to Protect Rivers and Waste Management
item.description: Environment Cabinet Secretary Deborah Barasa has instructed the National Environment Management Authority (NEMA) to strictly enforce environmental regulations across Kenya, emphasising that violators ...

item.title: The Impact of War on Ukraine’s Wildlife and Ecosystems
item.description: The ongoing war in Ukraine has severe consequences for wildlife, both direct and indirect. Approximately a third of Ukraine’s territory is mined, posing lethal risks not only to humans but also to ani...

item.title: Schedule and Key Details for Local Elections in England, Wales and Scotland
item.description: Voters in England, Wales and Scotland are heading to the polls on 7 May in the largest test for political parties since Labour's general election victory in July 2024. In England, almost 25,000 candid...

item.title: Australian women and children linked to IS expected to return home under legal scrutiny
item.description: A group of Australian women and children with alleged links to the Islamic State (IS) are set to return to Australia this Thursday night. The federal government has confirmed that 13 members, includin...

item.title: Reports of intimidation and vandalism surface in West Bengal after BJP election victory
item.description: Several incidents of alleged intimidation and vandalism were reported in West Bengal a day after the Bharatiya Janata Party won the Assembly elections. The reports, cited in the article, point to tens...

item.title: Rihanna and A$AP Rocky have private discussion after Met Gala
item.description: Rihanna, 38, and A$AP Rocky, 37, appeared to enjoy themselves at the Met Gala, exuding charm and playfulness on the red carpet, including humorous interactions with Heidi Klum. However, footage later ...

item.title: Kindiki Calls for Efficient Regulatory Services to Support Businesses and Livelihoods
item.description: Deputy President Kithure Kindiki has called on Kenya's regulatory authorities to operate more efficiently, fairly, and predictably to support economic growth and improve the lives of citizens. Speakin...

item.title: Kenya Disburses Over Sh2 Billion to Landowners for Electricity Transmission Projects
item.description: The Kenyan government has disbursed Sh2.23 billion as compensation to landowners affected by major electricity transmission projects, though Sh182 million is still pending. Energy CS Opiyo Wandayi rep...

item.title: JOOTRH Records Fewer Deaths After Expanding Specialist Services and Modern Equipment
item.description: At Jaramogi Oginga Odinga Teaching and Referral Hospital (JOOTRH) in Kisumu, patient deaths have gone down significantly following strategic investments in specialist care, modern equipment, and staff...
